label: code-wiki
name: Code Wiki (self-hosted Next.js documentation site for codebase maps)
summary: A generalized, self-hosted documentation wiki: a Next.js (App Router, standalone) app that ingests a codebases.json registry of {name, maps_dir} entries, transforms each codebase's docs/*_MAP.md into a browsable wiki (sidebar nav tree + markdown content pane + client-rendered Mermaid + dark/light theming), with no LLM, database, or second backend process. Deploys via the config-management phenotype.

trigger_phrases: launch a code wiki for my codebases
host the codebase maps in a visually appealing way
spin up a documentation wiki from the maps
i want a browsable wiki for our code documentation
render our codebase maps as a website

when_to_use: You have pre-generated codebase documentation (CT6 *_MAP.md or any markdown maps) and want a visually appealing, navigable, self-hosted wiki for one or many codebases.
You want Mermaid diagrams, a navigation tree, and dark/light theming without an LLM, a database, or a second backend process.
when_not: You need live LLM generation / Q&A over the code (that is the stripped capability; use deepwiki-open itself or the ai-management phenotype).
The content is not markdown maps (a different content shape needs a different loader).

contract_surface.registry: codebases.json — a JSON array of { name, maps_dir }; maps_dir holds docs/*_MAP.md
contract_surface.content_shape: WikiStructure { id, title, description, pages[], sections[], rootSections[] } + WikiPage { id, title, content(markdown w/ inline ```mermaid), filePaths[], importance, relatedPages[] }

parameters.name: wiki_name
parameters.description: display name of the wiki, e.g. 'Acme Engineering Docs'

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: HTTP port the wiki serves on

parameters.name: project_name
parameters.description: project/org prefix for cloud resource naming (iac)
